title: [MacOS M2 Pro v15.5] mkdir: Cannot create directory ‘/Users’: Read-only file system
body:
### Issue Description

When trying to start the default Podman machine (`podman machine start`), I am seeing the following errors:

```
chattr: Operation not supported while reading flags on /
mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/Users’: Read-only file system
chattr: Operation not supported while reading flags on /
```

However, the machine starts successfully and I am able to run images on them. But if I need to mount any files from the `/Users` folder, I get an error saying that the files cannot be found.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start with a fresh install of Podman on an Apple M2 Pro running Sequoia v15.5
2. Run `podman machine init -v $HOME:$HOME -v /private/tmp:/private/tmp -v /var/folders/:/var/folders/`
3. Run `podman machine start` and observe the above errors are displayed

### Describe the results you received

Output shows as follows:

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users/[REDACTED]:/Users/[REDACTED]
chattr: Operation not supported while reading flags on /
mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/Users’: Read-only file system
chattr: Operation not supported while reading flags on /
Error: exit status 1
```

### Describe the results you expected

Expected the provided folders to be mounted successfully, thereby allowing me to mount files when running containers

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
You don't show the client version and just from guessing you seem to be using an very outdated podman version on your mac. The log line you cite are not used on macos at all and we have been using systemd mount units inside the machine for a while now.

I guess you are using some old podman 4.X version that just pull the regular coreos instead of our new custom coreos build that set the root to rw.
